description:
A colleague has asked you to help prepare two customer-related CSV files for public release.  
    The workflow must (1) remove all Personally Identifiable Information (PII) and (2) verify in
    parallel that the cleaned files really are PII-free.

    Starting situation
    ------------------
    • Your working directory is /home/user  
    • Two raw CSV files already exist inside /home/user/raw_data

          /home/user/raw_data/customers.csv
          /home/user/raw_data/transactions.csv

    • You have no root privileges, but you do have the usual userland tools that ship with a
      modern Debian/Ubuntu distribution (grep, sed, awk, GNU parallel, find, etc.).  

    • A directory called /home/user/cleaned_data does NOT yet exist.

    Required end-state
    ------------------
    1. Create /home/user/cleaned_data and write *clean* versions of the two CSVs in it.  
       • Name them customers_clean.csv and transactions_clean.csv respectively.  
       • The clean versions **must NOT** contain any of the following fields (header or data):
            - email
            - ssn
            - card_number
            - cvv
       • All remaining columns must stay in their original order and retain every non-PII row.

    2. Produce a single, consolidated scan log that proves no residual PII is present.  
       • Path: /home/user/scan_logs/security_scan.log  
       • For reproducibility, the directory /home/user/scan_logs must be created by you.  

       • You must run the scans for the two cleaned CSVs *concurrently* (e.g. via GNU parallel,
         xargs -P 2, or background subshells).  The log should therefore show entries that could
         appear in any order; the test harness will sort internally before checking.

       • Each scan MUST append exactly one line to security_scan.log using the format:

             YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS | <filename> | <status> | <issues_found>

         where
         – YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS is the UTC timestamp of when that line was written  
         – <filename> is customers_clean.csv or transactions_clean.csv  
         – <status> is either OK or FAIL  
         – <issues_found> is an integer (0 when status=OK)  

       • A “PII issue” is defined as any text fragment matching either of the two regexes:
            1.   [0-9]{3}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{4}            (U.S. SSN)
            2.   [A-Z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Za-z]{2,}   (e-mail)

       • The finished log must therefore contain exactly two lines and both *should* read OK | 0
         once the cleaning is successful.

    3. For human auditing, place a short README at /home/user/cleaned_data/README.txt that
       explains in **two sentences maximum** what was removed and how the verification was done.
       No blank lines, no more than 160 characters in total.

    Verification rules the autograder will apply
    --------------------------------------------
    a) The directories /home/user/cleaned_data and /home/user/scan_logs both exist.  
    b) /home/user/cleaned_data contains the two *_clean.csv files whose header rows contain no
       banned field names and whose data rows contain no matches for either PII regex.  
    c) /home/user/scan_logs/security_scan.log exists, is non-empty, and contains exactly two
       well-formed lines that pass these checks:
          – Correct timestamp format: 4-digit year-month-day, space, 24-h time.  
          – Correct field separators (“space–pipe–space”).  
          – <filename> value is one of the two cleaned filenames.  
          – <status> equals OK.  
          – <issues_found> equals 0.  
    d) The scan must have been executed in *parallel*.  The autograder will parse shell history
       and process table snapshots to confirm at least two scanner processes overlapped in time
       (so do not run them strictly sequentially).  
    e) /home/user/cleaned_data/README.txt exists, is ≤160 characters total, has exactly two
       sentences, and mentions both “PII removal” and “regex” (case-insensitive check).

    Notes & Hints
    -------------
    • You may assume that customers.csv and transactions.csv are small, so in-memory processing
      with awk/sed is fine.  
    • Take care to preserve header capitalization and column order, merely delete the unwanted
      columns.  
    • Feel free to create temporary helper scripts or use process substitution; just keep them
      inside /home/user or /tmp so the agent can access them without sudo.  
    • The log file is append-only: each scanner appends its line, so concurrent writes must not
      overwrite each other.  (Using `>>` redirection is acceptable.)

    When everything is complete you should be able to run:

          grep -E '(email|ssn|card_number|cvv)' /home/user/cleaned_data/*.csv

    and receive zero output, while `cat /home/user/scan_logs/security_scan.log` shows two “OK”
    lines.  The autograder will perform equivalent checks.
truth:
Initial raw files that exist before the agent starts
    ----------------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/raw_data/customers.csv
       ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │ id,name,email,ssn,phone,country                                          │
       │ 1,Jane Doe,jane.doe@example.com,123-45-6789,555-1234,US                  │
       │ 2,Bob Smith,bob.smith@example.net,987-65-4321,555-2345,US               │
       └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    2. /home/user/raw_data/transactions.csv
       ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
       │ txn_id,card_number,cvv,amount,email,ssn                                  │
       │ T1001,4242424242424242,123,19.99,alice@data.org,000-12-3456             │
       │ T1002,4000056655665556,999,7.50,bob.smith@example.net,987-65-4321       │
       └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

    Expected state AFTER agent finishes
    -----------------------------------
    Directories:
      /home/user/cleaned_data        (mode 755)
      /home/user/scan_logs           (mode 755)

    Files & contents:

    a) /home/user/cleaned_data/customers_clean.csv
       Header: id,name,phone,country
       Row 1: 1,Jane Doe,555-1234,US
       Row 2: 2,Bob Smith,555-2345,US
       Exact newline format: LF, no trailing blank line.

    b) /home/user/cleaned_data/transactions_clean.csv
       Header: txn_id,amount
       Row 1: T1001,19.99
       Row 2: T1002,7.50
       Exact newline format: LF, no trailing blank line.

    c) /home/user/scan_logs/security_scan.log
       Two lines; order indeterminate.  Regex example:
         ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} \| customers_clean\.csv \| OK \| 0$
         ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2} \| transactions_clean\.csv \| OK \| 0$

    d) /home/user/cleaned_data/README.txt
       Must contain exactly two sentences, ≤160 characters total, and include both substrings
       “PII removal” and “regex”.  Example acceptable content (but agent may choose wording):

         PII removal deleted email, ssn, card_number and cvv columns. Verification used regex matching in parallel scans.

       (Length: 114 characters)

    Parallelism check the autograder will perform
    ---------------------------------------------
    During execution there must be overlapping runtimes for two grep/awk/sed processes that read
    the cleaned CSVs.  The presence of concurrently running PIDs, captured via `ps`, will be
    parsed by the test harness; overlap ≥1 s suffices.
